question: Does a confidence value derived ONLY from the STRUCTURE of evidence around a claim (independent-source count, source-type diversity, replication, consensus endorsement, recency, contradiction count) predict whether the claim is actually TRUE? This is a CONTRARIAN test of the identity axiom implicit in the OKF stack: topology_confidence == truth.
claimCeiling: candidate_only; canClaimAGI:false; the committed seed run is ILLUSTRATIVE (underpowered), never a validated result
direction: axiom predicts rho > 0 (more/stronger/less-contradicted evidence => higher truth)
requiredNComputed: tools/eval_stats.required_n_for_mde(0.30) at 80% power, alpha 0.05 (computed at run time and echoed into the receipt as requiredN); the seed set below is deliberately smaller than this

noGoRule.rule: rho <= 0 (at power) FALSIFIES the identity axiom
noGoRule.consequence: if falsified, DO NOT assume topology == truth; SHIP AN EMPIRICAL topology->truth CALIBRATION LAYER (learn the mapping from evidence structure to truth-probability from an externally-labeled corpus) instead of assuming identity

uncertaintyRule: the association carries a deterministic seeded PERMUTATION p-value (tools/stats_ext.permutation_pvalue, alternative='greater'); at this tiny binary-outcome N the asymptotic Spearman t-approximation is untrustworthy, so no parametric p is claimed
magnitudeRule: GO requires BOTH rho > 0 AND p <= alpha in the axiom's predicted direction AND n >= requiredN; a positive but non-significant, or underpowered, rho is NOT a GO

powerRule.rule: the probe refuses to emit a directional axiom verdict unless n >= requiredN (measurement-thesis Pillar 2); underpowered => verdict 'UNDERPOWERED' (exit 3), which neither confirms nor falsifies the axiom

underpoweredNote: The committed seed set (agi-proof/topology-truth-axiom/labeled_set.jsonl, ~25 claims) is SMALL, ILLUSTRATIVE, and UNDERPOWERED. It exercises the REAL harness end-to-end and shows the plumbing works, but it CANNOT validate or falsify the axiom. A powered test needs a larger EXTERNALLY-labeled set (>= requiredN) drawn beyond the handful of myths/facts the wiki already catalogs, with truth values fixed by sources outside this probe and evidence features assigned blind to the truth label.
decontamRule: the topology_confidence formula (tools/run_topology_truth_probe._WEIGHTS) is a FIXED, label-free monotone prior — it is NOT fit to the truth labels; fitting to labels would make the probe circular and is a hard NO-GO

groundTruth.rule: each claim's truth value must be EXTERNALLY fixed (a cataloged wiki myth/fact page, or standing scientific/historical consensus) and recorded per-row in truthProvenance; author-only truth judgements do NOT satisfy this

noCircularFit.rule: the evidence-structure -> confidence map must be pre-committed and label-free; deriving weights from the truth labels voids the run

noSeedSetClaim.rule: the underpowered seed-set rho may NEVER be presented as a validated confirmation OR falsification of the axiom; it is illustrative harness output only

protectedDomains.rule: religion and history claims may appear as labeled items but the probe proposes NO merges / same-lineage / impersonation edges; doNotMergeWith and doNotAttributeTo remain hard constraints

topology.primaryMetric: spearman_rho(topology_confidence, revealed_truth) with seeded permutation p

preRegistrationProof: This spec fixes the rho<=0 NO-GO, the alpha, the MDE, the power gate, and the no-circular-fit / no-seed-set-claim guardrails BEFORE any powered externally-labeled run exists, so the criteria cannot be tuned to the result. tools/claim_gate.py --assert-prereg can verify this spec's commit predates any future powered result.
honestBound: Pre-registration only. NO powered run has executed. The committed seed run is REAL harness output on a small illustrative set and is UNDERPOWERED by construction (n < requiredN). A powered NO-GO (rho <= 0) is a valid, publishable outcome and would redirect the OKF stack from an assumed identity to an empirically-calibrated topology->truth layer.
